                  Revision of Seasonally Adjusted Labor Force Series

              The Bureau of Labor Statistics pwh year revises the       5 years only; thus series for the years 1967-69 were not
           seasonal adjustment factors for unemployment and other       subject to revision. To maintain a partial historical
           labor force series derived from the Current Population       record, the seasonally adjusted series have been pub-
           Survey (household survey) to take into account data          lished in this issue for 1967 forward. Pre-1967 data,
           from the previous year. This issue of Employment and         which have been adjusted by the BLS Seasonal Factor
           Earnings introduces revised seasonally adjusted data         Method, 2 are also no longer subject to change. Users of
           which incorporate experience through December 1974.          these data who retain the February 1973 issue of this
           The revised seasonally adjusted factors shown on page        publication have a permanent source of pre-1967 sea-
            9 and the seasonally adjusted series shown on pages         sonally adjusted data. These historical data may also be
           132-180 replace those published in the February 1974         obtained from the BLS upon request.
           issue of this publication, which were based on data
           through December 1973.                                       Method of adjustment
               Incorporation of 1974 data and recomputation of
           the seasonally adjusted data did not affect the published,       The X-11 seasonal adjustment method is an adapta-
           seasonally adjusted unemployment rates for 10 months         tion of the traditional ratio-to-moving average method,
           of 1974 and altered them by 0.1 percentage point m the       with allowance for changing seasonal patterns. The
           remaining 2 months. Because they are calculated from         original data are regarded as a product of a trend-cycle
            12 monthly observations based on unadjusted numbers,        component, a seasonal component, and an irregular
           annual averages are not affected by the revision of sea-     component. The trend-cycle represents the long-run
                                                                        trend and cycle movements of the series. The seasonal
           sonal factors.
                                                                        component is the annual repetitive pattern which makes
               As has been the case since 1973, the Census Bureau's
                                                                        certain months consistently higher or lower than others.
            X-11 method 1 was used to seasonally adjust iabor force
                                                                        The irregular component is a residual, including sampling
           data. For most series, except those pertaining to veterans
                                                                        errors and short-term fluctuations due to unusual
           and non-veterans, seasonality has been computed based
                                                                        weather, strikes, etc., which do not follow any consistent
           on the prior 8-year period. However, beginning with this
                                                                        pattern After a satisfactory decomposition is achieved,
           year, revisions of seasonally adjusted data are limited to

                                                                        each month's original value by the corresponding seasonal
